classification_rationale: This repo is the canonical JSON Schema + spec library that defines the AFI interoperability surface. README.md:3-5 declares it the "single source of truth for all AFI Protocol configuration schemas". It physically holds named normative artifacts: USS v1/v1.1 (schemas/usignal/), CPJ v0.1 (schemas/cpj/v0_1/), vault.schema.json, pipeline.schema.json, blueprint.schema.json, plugin-manifest.schema.json, and the REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md invariants spec. AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:15 correctly self-identifies these as "normative (protocol law)" vs reference implementations. These are the schemas an external validator must conform to, so the repo is NORMATIVE rather than a reference impl.
primary_purpose: afi-config is the canonical configuration and JSON Schema library for AFI Protocol: it provides the normative ingest dialects (USS v1.1, CPJ v0.1), vault/pipeline/plugin/validator/blueprint schemas, agent-registry + reputation invariants, and AJV-based validation utilities consumed by every other AFI module.

reference_only_assumptions.quote: **`afi-reactor`** is the orchestrator; do not reintroduce or reference `afi-engine`.
Schema for Time-Series Signal Data Vault configurations in afi-infra
Schema for signal processing pipeline configurations in afi-reactor
Schema for plugin manifests in afi-plugins registry
Schema for analyst configurations in the AFI-Reactor pipeline / DAG integration.
Enrichment nodes are processing units that transform, analyze, or augment signals within the AFI-Reactor pipeline / DAG.

contradictions.title: Vault schema lacks canonical lifecycle stage enum despite stages being a normative protocol concept
contradictions.detail: The portable-protocol north star defines a per-signal RAW->ENRICHED->ANALYZED->SCORED->MINTED->REPLAYED lifecycle that an external stack must reproduce. vault.schema.json only mentions stages as a free-text index hint and defines no enumerated `stage` field or stage transition rules anywhere in schemas/. The lifecycle is therefore not machine-enforceable from the canonical schema library, weakening interoperability/replay guarantees.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/vault.schema.json:95-98 "stageIndex": {..."description": "Create index on signal stage (RAW, ENRICHED, etc.)"} — no enum/stage field defined elsewhere

contradictions.title: No on-chain commitment schema in the canonical library
contradictions.detail: Vault enumerates Mongo/Postgres/Timescale/Influx as the evidence plane (MongoDB TSSD is the implemented evidence store) but the library has zero schemas for the Commitment plane (mint receipts/emissions caps/epoch linkage/content hashes). grep for kafka|warehouse|snowflake|mintForSignal|EmissionsMinted|AFISignalReceipt returns no schema hits; no separate analytics/warehouse plane is planned for the reference implementation. The commitment plane is not represented in the source of truth, risking collapse of the layer model.
contradictions.evidence: grep kafka|warehouse|snowflake across repo: 0 hits; grep mintForSignal|coordinateMint|EmissionsMinted: only validatorConfig coordinatorAddress + droid-index doc mentions, no schema

contradictions.title: Charter hard-pins afi-reactor as THE orchestrator (reference-impl-as-law)
contradictions.detail: AFI_DROID_CHARTER.v0.1.md is declared globally binding (AGENTS.md:5 'If this AGENTS.md conflicts with the Charter, the Charter wins') and states afi-reactor IS the orchestrator and forbids referencing alternatives. This collides with AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:15 which calls afi-reactor a 'reference implementation, not mandatory stack'. Within one repo the reference spine is simultaneously declared optional (overview) and mandated identity (charter).
contradictions.evidence: codex/governance/droids/AFI_DROID_CHARTER.v0.1.md:117 "**`afi-reactor`** is the orchestrator" vs docs/AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:15 "afi-reactor ... are reference implementations—demonstration spines, not mandatory stacks"

contradictions.title: Schema descriptions hard-bind generic schemas to specific org repos
contradictions.detail: Generic, portable schemas (vault, pipeline, plugin-manifest, analyst-config, enrichment-node) describe themselves as belonging to afi-infra / afi-reactor / afi-plugins. An external validator reading the canonical schema cannot tell the rule from the reference deployment, blurring the normative/reference line the protocol depends on.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/pipeline.schema.json:5 "...pipeline configurations in afi-reactor"; schemas/vault.schema.json:5 "...Vault configurations in afi-infra"

contradictions.title: Orphaned .afi-codex.schema.json contradicts the actual codex metadata schema
contradictions.detail: README.md:29 and AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:153 advertise `.afi-codex.schema.json` as the 'Codex metadata schema (all repos)'. But that file is a persona/abilities schema requiring [persona, abilities]; the real .afi-codex.json (codexVersion 2.0.0, module/entrypoints/provides) is validated against schemas/repo-metadata.schema.json instead (test line 141). The advertised codex schema is stale and would reject every real .afi-codex.json.

contradictions.title: CI never runs schema validation (script name mismatch)
contradictions.detail: The CI gate calls `npm run validate:config --if-present` and `npm run build --if-present`, but package.json defines `validate` and `build`, not `validate:config`. The validate step silently falls through to 'No validation script', so PRs to the single-source-of-truth schema repo merge without the AJV compile/example tests running. Normative schema changes are effectively ungated.
contradictions.evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml:21-22 "run: npm run validate:config --if-present || echo \"No validation script\"" vs package.json:13 "validate": "vitest run tests/schema-validation.test.ts"

contradictions.title: Dual USS canon (v1 vs v1.1) without a normative supersession statement
contradictions.detail: USS v1 (provenance requires only timestamp) and USS v1.1 (provenance requires source+providerId+signalId, the audit/reward keys) coexist. vault.schema.json and pipeline.schema.json still default signalSchema to 'afi.usignal.v1', while v1.1 is labeled 'living canon for runtime ingestion'. No doc states which is binding for replay/attribution, so an external implementer could validate against the weaker v1 and omit signalId/providerId.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/usignal/v1/index.schema.json:207-209 provenance required:[timestamp] vs schemas/usignal/v1_1/index.schema.json:235-239 required:[source,providerId,signalId]; vault.schema.json:43 default "afi.usignal.v1"

contradictions.title: Duplicated, divergent USS schemas in afi-uss-pr-v2p1/ staging directory
contradictions.detail: afi-uss-pr-v2p1/ ships a parallel copy of USS v1 schemas + TS types under schema/usignal/v1/ that diverges from the canonical schemas/usignal/v1/ (different $schema draft: 2020-12 vs draft-07, no $id). A second physical 'source of truth' for the same dialect inside the canonical repo invites drift and confuses consumers about which path is authoritative.

notable_findings.title: USS v1.1 provenance carries the replay/attribution determinism keys
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/usignal/v1_1/index.schema.json:235-261 provenance required [source,providerId,signalId]; properties ingestHash "SHA256 hash of raw payload ... for integrity verification", facts block (line 33) "replay-canonical market/strategy metadata ... persisted in TSSD vault for deterministic replay"
notable_findings.recommendation: Treat v1.1 index+core as the binding ingest dialect for replay; tests already enforce providerId/signalId rejection (test lines 461-505). Add a doc note marking v1.1 as the canon for replay/attribution and v1 as legacy.

notable_findings.title: Vault schema correctly supports multi-engine (refutes Mongo-only tunnel vision)
notable_findings.recommendation: Good alignment with portable protocol. Strengthen further by adding an enumerated signal lifecycle `stage` field so the RAW->REPLAYED canon is engine-agnostic and machine-checkable.

notable_findings.title: REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1 codifies the determinism guardrail exactly as the north star requires
notable_findings.evidence: docs/REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md:104-118 "Reputation cannot alter the Unified Weighting Rule (UWR)...", "Vault finality (T.S.S.D. Vault immutability) is absolute; reputation cannot rewrite history"
notable_findings.recommendation: No change; this is the canonical statement of the reputation-MUST-NOT-override-determinism rule. Cross-link it from the portable-protocol surface spec.

notable_findings.title: CPJ v0.1 is a true normative ingest dialect with parser provenance + confidence
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/cpj/v0_1/core.schema.json:5 "CPJ is source-faithful parsed object + provenance + parse confidence"; index.schema.json:5 "first normalization stage before USS v1.1 mapping"
notable_findings.recommendation: Keep. CPJ->USS v1.1 mapping is replay-relevant; ensure the mapping itself (CPJ to USS) is pinned/versioned somewhere normative.

notable_findings.title: CI validation gate is a no-op due to script name mismatch
notable_findings.evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml:21-22 run: npm run validate:config --if-present (package.json has no validate:config; defines validate at line 13)
notable_findings.recommendation: Fix CI to call `npm run validate` (and `npm test`) so the AJV schema-compile + example-validation suite gates every PR to the source-of-truth repo. Until fixed, malformed normative schemas can merge to main.

notable_findings.title: Advertised .afi-codex.schema.json is orphaned/stale vs repo-metadata.schema.json
notable_findings.evidence: README.md:29 lists ".afi-codex.schema.json - Codex metadata schema (all repos)"; file requires [persona,abilities] (.afi-codex.schema.json:31-34) but .afi-codex.json is validated against schemas/repo-metadata.schema.json (test:141)
notable_findings.recommendation: Either delete/repurpose .afi-codex.schema.json or repoint README/overview to repo-metadata.schema.json as the actual codex metadata schema. Add a test that validates .afi-codex.json against whatever is advertised.

notable_findings.title: Generic schemas hard-label themselves to specific org repos
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/pipeline.schema.json:5 "...in afi-reactor"; schemas/vault.schema.json:5 "...in afi-infra"; schemas/plugin-manifest.schema.json:5 "...in afi-plugins registry"; schemas/definitions/enrichment-node.schema.json:5 "...within the AFI-Reactor pipeline / DAG"
notable_findings.recommendation: Rephrase descriptions to be stack-neutral (e.g., 'pipeline/DAG configuration for any conforming orchestrator; afi-reactor is the reference implementation'). This is the cheapest, highest-leverage normative/reference clarification.

notable_findings.title: Charter (globally binding) declares afi-reactor THE orchestrator, conflicting with overview
notable_findings.evidence: AFI_DROID_CHARTER.v0.1.md:117 "afi-reactor is the orchestrator"; AGENTS.md:5 "the Charter wins"; AFI_CONFIG_OVERVIEW.md:15 afi-reactor is "reference implementation ... not mandatory"
notable_findings.recommendation: Reconcile: scope the Charter's 'reactor is the orchestrator' to the reference deployment/org-internal work, and explicitly state external validators may run any conforming orchestrator that emits USS/TSSD-compliant outputs.

notable_findings.title: No Commitment-plane schema (mint receipt/emissions/epoch/content-hash anchors)
notable_findings.evidence: Only validatorConfig.schema.json:79-103 references mint (coordinatorAddress regex, chainId, gas) and droid docs name AFIMintCoordinator/AFISignalReceipt/EmissionsMinted; no schema defines receipt metadata (signalId/epoch/amounts/beneficiary/contentHash)
notable_findings.recommendation: Add a normative receipt/anchor schema to this library so the on-chain<->off-chain linkage (signalId/epoch/content hash) is validatable by external parties, per decentralization rule 4. Even a breadcrumb-receipt schema would close the gap.

notable_findings.title: Duplicate divergent USS copy under afi-uss-pr-v2p1/
notable_findings.evidence: afi-uss-pr-v2p1/schema/usignal/v1/core.schema.json uses draft 2020-12 and omits $id; canonical schemas/usignal/v1/core.schema.json uses draft-07 with $id; diff returns DIFFERENT
notable_findings.recommendation: Remove the afi-uss-pr-v2p1/ staging directory (or fold useful greeks updates into canonical schemas) so there is one authoritative USS path. It is unreferenced by tests and not under schemas/.

notable_findings.title: validatorConfig encodes mint/governance/challenge-window determinism inputs
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/validatorConfig.schema.json:7-46 required [decayCheckIntervalMs, challengeWindowDurationHours, mintApprovalThreshold, snapshotSpaceId]; mint.chainId comment "8453 for Base"; coordinatorAddress regex ^0x[a-fA-F0-9]{40}$
notable_findings.recommendation: These thresholds are replay/finality-relevant. Ensure validator replay spec pins these values per-epoch so a different stack reproduces the same mint/no_mint decision.
